heading: Modules
text: Several modules can used run LandR depending user’s needs. LandRCBM, five different modules frequently used. brief description one.

heading: 1 Biomass_yieldTables
text: documentation work progress. Potential discrepancies omissions may exist time . find , please contact us .

heading: 1.1 Overview
text: Biomass_yieldTables produces biomass yield curves cohorts user-specified study area LandR. module initialize landscape keeping species composition, setting biomass 1 ages 0. module Biomass_core run internally without dispersal disturbances simulate vegetation dynamics year 0 species dies (.e., maximum longevity among species). module records aboveground biomass species, pixel, time step build yield tables take account species traits competition.

heading: 1.2 Inputs
text: module needs inputs run Biomass_core. now, defaults inputs, user either need provide inputs run module alongside modules can generate expected inputs (e.g., Biomass_borealDataPrep).

heading: 1.3.1 Events
text: module one event, init event. , module initializes landscape, keeping appropriate species present pixel setting ages 0 biomass 1. New pixel groups created pixels share species composition ecoregion, groups become yieldTableIndex. Vegetation dynamics simulated running Biomass_core internally cohortData object saved year. cohortData objects year loaded combined create yield tables, plotted saved ouputs folder.

heading: 1.3.3 Usage
text: example workflow build yield tables Western Boreal Regional Integrated Assessment (RIA) study area. Note need data permissions permanent sample plot inventory data run global. permissions, sure sign appropriate Google account. Note global requires large files initial run take time download .
code:
repos <- c("predictiveecology.r-universe.dev", getOption("repos"))
if (!require("SpaDES.project")){
  Require::Install(c("SpaDES.project", "SpaDES.core", "reproducible"), repos = repos, dependencies = TRUE)
}

getRIA <- function(x) {
  x <- sf::st_read(x) 
  ria <- x[x$TSA_NUMBER %in% c('08', '16', '24', '40', '41'),]
  ria <- sf::st_union(ria)|> sf::st_as_sf()
  return(ria)
}

out <- SpaDES.project::setupProject(
  paths = list(projectPath = getwd(),
               inputPath = "~/inputs",
               outputPath = "outputs/yieldTables",
               cachePath = "cache"),
  options = options(spades.moduleCodeChecks = FALSE,
                    spades.recoveryMode = FALSE),
  times = list(start = 2020, end = 2020),
  modules = c("PredictiveEcology/Biomass_speciesFactorial@development",
              "PredictiveEcology/Biomass_borealDataPrep@development",
              "PredictiveEcology/Biomass_speciesParameters@development",
              "PredictiveEcology/Biomass_yieldTables@main"
  ),
  params = list(
    .globals = list(
      .plots = c("png"),
      .plotInterval = 10,
      sppEquivCol = 'LandR',
      .studyAreaName = "RIAsmall"
    ),
    Biomass_borealDataPrep = list(
      .studyAreaName = "RIA",
      subsetDataBiomassModel = 50
    ),
    Biomass_speciesFactorial = list(
      .plots = NULL, #"pdf",
      runExperiment = TRUE,
      factorialSize = "medium"
    ),
    Biomass_speciesParameters = list(
      .plots = "png",
      standAgesForFitting = c(0, 110),
      .useCache = c(".inputObjects", "init"),
      speciesFittingApproach = "focal"
    ),
    Biomass_yieldTables = list(
      moduleNameAndBranch = "PredictiveEcology/Biomass_core@development",
      maxAge = 150,
      .plots = "png",
      .useCache = "generateData"
    )
  ),
  packages = c("googledrive", 'RCurl', 'XML', "stars"),
  useGit = F,
  functions = getRIA,
  # Study area is within RIA (RIA buffered 150km inward)
  studyArea = {
    reproducible::prepInputs(
      url = "https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LxacDOobTrRUppamkGgVAUFIxNT4iiHU/view?usp=sharing",
      destinationPath = "inputs",
      fun = getRIA,
      overwrite = TRUE
    ) |> sf::st_buffer(dist = -150000)
  },
  studyAreaLarge = studyArea,
  rasterToMatch = {
    sa <- terra::vect(studyArea)
    targetCRS <- terra::crs(sa)
    rtm <- terra::rast(sa, res = c(250, 250), crs = targetCRS)
    rtm[] <- 1
    rtm <- terra::mask(rtm, sa)
    rtm
  },
  sppEquiv = {
    speciesInStudy <- LandR::speciesInStudyArea(studyAreaLarge,
                                                dPath = "inputs")
    species <- LandR::equivalentName(speciesInStudy$speciesList, df = LandR::sppEquivalencies_CA, "LandR")
    sppEquiv <- LandR::sppEquivalencies_CA[LandR %in% species]
    sppEquiv <- sppEquiv[KNN != "" & LANDIS_traits != ""] #avoid a bug with shore pine
  }
)

out$loadOrder <- unlist(out$modules)

initOut <- SpaDES.core::simInit2(out)
simOut <- SpaDES.core::spades(initOut)

heading: 2.1 Overview
text: LandRCBM_split3pools translates forest landscape dynamics simulated LandR compatible spadesCBM. module provides annual increments three ground biomass pools: merchantable, foliage, . yield tables used spinup event spadesCBM, annual cohort data used annual event spadesCBM.

heading: 2.2 Inputs
text: inputs LandRCBM_split3pools combination outputs LandR, data convert total ground biomass biomass merchantable, foliage wood pools, outputs spadesCBM.default inputs Western Boreal Regional Integrated Assessment (RIA) study area. inputs expected provided modules (CBMdefault, Biomass_core, Biomass_yieldTables, Biomass_borealDataPrep, etc.).

heading: 2.3.1 Events
text: LandRCBM_split3pools 7 events, 3 plotting events. Saved plots can found ~/LandRCBM/outputs/[simulation name]/figures/LandRCBM_split3pools directory.

heading: 2.3.1.1 splitInit
text: initial event splits total ground biomass yield tables initial landscape biomass ground carbon pools (merchantable, foliage, ).

heading: 2.3.1.2 postSpinupAdjustBiomass
text: CBM spinup, biomass aboveground (merchantable, foliage, ) belowground (coarse roots fine roots) live carbon pools match observed data. event re-initializes pools match observed initial aboveground biomass.

heading: 2.3.1.3 annualIncrements
text: event, cohortData current simulation year compared previous year difference biomass cohort calculated converted increments three aboveground carbon pools.

heading: 2.3.1.4 prepareCBMvars
text: year, object cbm_vars updated incorporate calculated annual aboveground increments, handle dead organic matter cohorts (cohort without aboveground biomass), new cohorts, disturbed cohorts.

heading: 2.3.1.5 summarizeAGBPools
text: , ground biomass ground carbon pool summarized species across landscape time step.

heading: 2.3.1.6 plotYC
text: event plots yield curves number randomly selected pixel groups. yield curves describe aboveground carbon pools change stand age.

heading: 2.3.1.7 plotMaps
text: event maps aboveground carbon stocks increments regular interval.

heading: 2.3.1.8 plotSummaries
text: simple plotting event plots evolution aboveground biomass pool species entire landscape end simulation.

heading: 2.3.2 Estimating merchantable proportion of stemwood
text:
Merchantable wood estimated Boudewyn et al. (2007) following equation:\(nonmerchfactor = k + * b_m^b\)
\(nonmerchfactor = b_{nm}/b_m\)
\(b_{nm} = b_m + b_n\):\(b_n\) stemwood biomass live, nonmerchantable trees\(b_n\) stemwood biomass live, nonmerchantable trees\(b_m\) stemwood biomass merchantable sized live trees\(b_m\) stemwood biomass merchantable sized live trees\(b_{nm}\) total stemwood biomass live trees\(b_{nm}\) total stemwood biomass live trees\(k\), \(\), \(b\) model parameters fit separately jurisdiction, ecozone, lead tree species (Table 4 Boudewyn et al. (2007)).\(k\), \(\), \(b\) model parameters fit separately jurisdiction, ecozone, lead tree species (Table 4 Boudewyn et al. (2007)).possible analytically infer new parameters equation , inferred simulations resulting new exponential model form estimate mechantable proportion stemwood:\(b_m = k - e^{-(b_{nm} - b)}\):\(k\), \(\), \(b\) model parameters inferred separately Boudewyn et al. (2007)value \(b_m\) exceeds simulated inferred \(cap\), value default \(cap\) proportion like :\[\text{b_m} =
\begin{cases}
\text{b_m}, & \text{b_m} > \text{cap} \\
\text{cap}, & \text{b_m} \le \text{cap}
\end{cases}\]merchantable proportion stemwood biomass calculated way species present simulation. example species along comparison estimates using original Boudewyn et al. (2007) equation.figure 1: comparison merchantable proportion curves fitted function used Biomass_yieldTables equations Boudewyn et al. (2007) number species

heading: Running the simulation
text: R script LandRCBM simulation small area British Columbia 2020 2022 simulated disturbance. Note need access permanent sample plot inventory data run global currently publicly available.
code:
projectPath <- "~/LandRCBM"
repos <- c("predictiveecology.r-universe.dev", getOption("repos"))
if (!require("SpaDES.project")){
  Require::Install(c("SpaDES.project"), repos = repos, dependencies = TRUE)
}

getRIA <- function(x) {
  x <- sf::st_read(x) 
  ria <- x[x$TSA_NUMBER %in% c('08', '16', '24', '40', '41'),]
  ria <- sf::st_union(ria)|> sf::st_as_sf()
  return(ria)
}

out <- SpaDES.project::setupProject(
  overwrite = FALSE,
  paths = list(projectPath = projectPath,
               outputPath  = file.path(projectPath, "outputs", "tinyRIA"),
               modulePath  = file.path(projectPath, "modules"),
               packagePath = file.path(projectPath, "packages"),
               inputPath   = file.path(projectPath, "inputs"),
               cachePath   = file.path(projectPath, "cache")),
  options = options(
    repos = c(repos = repos),
    Require.cloneFrom = Sys.getenv("R_LIBS_USER"),
    spades.moduleCodeChecks = FALSE,
    spades.recoveryMode = FALSE,
    reproducible.useMemoise = TRUE),
  times = list(start = 2020, end = 2022),
  modules = c(
    "PredictiveEcology/Biomass_speciesFactorial@development",
    "PredictiveEcology/Biomass_borealDataPrep@development",
    "PredictiveEcology/Biomass_speciesParameters@development",
    "PredictiveEcology/CBM_defaults@main",
    "PredictiveEcology/Biomass_yieldTables@main",
    "PredictiveEcology/Biomass_core@main",
    "PredictiveEcology/CBM_dataPrep@main",
    "PredictiveEcology/LandRCBM_split3pools@main",
    "PredictiveEcology/CBM_core@main"
  ),
  packages = c("googledrive", 'RCurl', 'XML', "stars", "httr2", "terra"),
  studyArea = {
    reproducible::prepInputs(
      url = "https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LxacDOobTrRUppamkGgVAUFIxNT4iiHU/view?usp=sharing",
      destinationPath = "~/inputs",
      fun = getRIA,
      overwrite = TRUE
    ) |> sf::st_crop(c(xmin = 1000000, xmax = 1200000, ymin = 1100000, ymax = 1300000))
  },
  studyArea_biomassParam = studyArea,
  rasterToMatch = {
    sa <- terra::vect(studyArea)
    targetCRS <- terra::crs(sa)
    rtm <- terra::rast(sa, res = c(250, 250))
    terra::crs(rtm) <- targetCRS
    rtm[] <- 1
    rtm <- terra::mask(rtm, sa)
    rtm
  },
  masterRaster = {
    masterRaster = rasterToMatch
    masterRaster
  },
  rasterToMatch_biomassParam = masterRaster, 
  sppEquiv = {
    speciesInStudy <- LandR::speciesInStudyArea(studyArea,
                                                dPath = "~/inputs")
    species <- LandR::equivalentName(speciesInStudy$speciesList, df = LandR::sppEquivalencies_CA, "LandR")
    sppEquiv <- LandR::sppEquivalencies_CA[LandR %in% species]
    sppEquiv <- sppEquiv[KNN != "" & LANDIS_traits != ""] #avoid a bug with shore pine
  },
  params = list(
    .globals = list(
      dataYear = 2020,
      .plots = c("png"),
      .plotInterval = 10,
      sppEquivCol = 'LandR',
      .studyAreaName = "RIA"
    ),
    Biomass_core = list(.plots = NA),
    Biomass_borealDataPrep = list(
      .studyAreaName = "RIA",
      subsetDataBiomassModel = 50
    ),
    Biomass_yieldTables = list(
      moduleNameAndBranch = "PredictiveEcology/Biomass_core@development",
      maxAge = 200,
      .plots = "png",
      .useCache = "generateData"
    ),
    Biomass_speciesParameters = list(
      .plots = "png",
      standAgesForFitting = c(0, 110),
      .useCache = c(".inputObjects", "init"),
      speciesFittingApproach = "focal"
    ),
    CBM_core = list(
      skipPrepareCBMvars = TRUE
    )
  )
)

out$loadOrder <- unlist(out$modules)

initOut <- SpaDES.core::simInit2(out)
simOut <- SpaDES.core::spades(initOut)
